title: Bridge Essay: Evaluating brain spectral and connectivity differences between silent mind-wande

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Formless/Absorption** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Formless absorption — meditative absorption, samadhi, jhana, trance states, transcendental meditation, non-ordinary states of consciousness.

**Evaluating brain spectral and connectivity differences between silent mind-wandering and trance states.** — Prog Brain Res. PMID: 37301570. DOI: 10.1016/bs.pbr.2022.12.011. Trance is an altered state of consciousness characterized by alterations in cognition. In general, trance states induce mental silence (i.e., cognitive thought reduction), and mental silence can induce trance states. Conversely, mind-wandering is the mind'spropensity to stray its attention away from the task at hand and toward content irrelevant to the current moment, and its main component is inner speech.
